description:
## Goal

Add a way for an admin to dump the Crystal GC heap to a file
on demand, so we can see what's actually accumulating in
memory (the 4.3 GB / 80 GB VIRT after 22h).

## Why

We have a real memory leak — 50 KB/sec sustained over 22h.
The candidates I've identified in the code review are all
plausible, but without a heap dump we can't tell which is the
real culprit. The cheapest way to find out is to dump the
heap, then `strings` + `sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head` to
see what's most prevalent.

## Design

Add a `def_call` to `MemoryManagerActor` that runs inside the
actor (so the heap dump captures a consistent snapshot):

```crystal
def_call dump_heap, String  # returns the path
```

Implementation: writes to `/tmp/qh-heap-<timestamp>.dump` and
returns the path.

Add a `QuickHeadlines::Controllers::AdminController#heap_dump`
endpoint at `GET /api/admin/heap_dump` (auth required) that
calls this and returns the path. The user can then `scp` the
file off the box and analyze it.

## Acceptance

- `GET /api/admin/heap_dump` (with admin auth) returns
  `{"path": "/tmp/qh-heap-...dump"}` and the file exists
- The dump file is parseable by Crystal tools (`strings | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn` shows the most common strings)
- Build + spec + ameba clean
- Boot smoke test: hitting the endpoint doesn't crash the
  server
title: Add admin endpoint to dump Crystal GC heap
